LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF PHILIP E. KAY'S MOTION TO VACATE RULINGS AND/OR ORDERS ON DEMURRERS, ISSUED ON MARCH 2, 2011, AND HEAR THE RE-NOTICED MOTION TO AMEND AND FILE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. AFTER HEARING, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO VACATE RULINGS AND/OR ORDER ON DEMURRERS ISSUED ON MARCH 2, 2011 DENIED. WHILE PLAINTIFF WAS FREE TO TAKE OFF CALENDAR HIS THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT, THE COURT SPECIFICALLY CONTINUED THE HEARING ON THE DEMURRERS TO THE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR FINAL DECISION ON THAT DATE WHICH PLAINTIFF DID NOT SEEK A CONTINUANCE OF BY MOTION TO COURT. PLAINTIFF ALSO FAILED TO CHECK THE COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING BY EITHER TELEPHONE OR ON-LINE. FURTHERMORE, THE COURT ORIGINALLY CONTINUED ITS FINAL DECISION ON THE DEMURRERS TO 3/2/11 AS A COURTESY TO PLAINTIFF WHO, JUST PRIOR TO THE HEARING ON THE DEMURRERS, FILED A MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE THE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT MADE IT VERY CLEAR THAT THE DEMURRERS WOULD BE DECIDED ON MARCH 2, 2011. ACCORDINGLY THE COURT DENIES PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO VACATE THE RULING ON THE DEMURRERS. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. (302)

Off Calendar Apr-08-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF PHILIP E. KAY'S MOTION TO VACATE RULINGS AND/OR ORDERS ON DEMURRERS, ISSUED ON MARCH 2, 2011, AND HEAR THE RE-NOTICED MOTION TO AMEND AND FILE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT.

APPEARANCES: Philip E. Kay, 387-6622 pltf & moving party in propria persona;
Michael Von Loewenfeldt, Esq. of Kerr & Wagstaffe LLP 371-8909 for defts THE STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA, BOARD OF GOVERNORS OF THE STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA, THE OFFICE OF THE CHIEF TRIAL COUNSEL, LUCY ARMENDARIZ, SCOTT J. DREXEL, JEFF DAL CERRO, AND ALLEN BLUMENTHAL;
Harry T. Gower, III, Deputy Atty General, Office of the Attorney General 703-1336 for defts SUPREME COURT OF CALIFORNIA, CHIEF JUSTICE RONALD M. GEORGE, JUDGE MICHAEL M. ANELLO, AND JUDGE JOAN P. WEBER.

RULING: Argued and the Court took the matter under submission.
After hearing, the Court ruled as follows:
MOTION TO VACATE RULINGS AND/OR ORDER ON DEMURRERS ISSUED ON MARCH 2, 2011 DENIED. WHILE PLAINTIFF WAS FREE TO TAKE OFF CALENDAR HIS THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT, THE COURT SPECIFICALLY CONTINUED THE HEARING ON THE DEMURRERS TO THE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR FINAL DECISION ON THAT DATE WHICH PLAINTIFF DID NOT SEEK A CONTINUANCE OF BY MOTION TO COURT. PLAINTIFF ALSO FAILED TO CHECK THE COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING BY EITHER TELEPHONE OR ON-LINE. FURTHERMORE, THE COURT ORIGINALLY CONTINUED ITS FINAL DECISION ON THE DEMURRERS TO 3/2/11 AS A COURTESY TO PLAINTIFF WHO, JUST PRIOR TO THE HEARING ON THE DEMURRERS, FILED A MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE THE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT MADE IT VERY CLEAR THAT THE DEMURRERS WOULD BE DECIDED ON MARCH 2, 2011. ACCORDINGLY THE COURT DENIES PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO VACATE THE RULING ON THE DEMURRERS. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
